description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular agricultural building with a prominent gable roof clad in reddish-brown material. The structure features light tan wooden siding and a large overhanging roof that provides shelter along one side. A wooden staircase leads up to a raised entry door at one end, while the opposite end displays two rectangular windows. The main facade includes several large, horizontally-aligned barn doors, likely for livestock access and ventilation. The building is elevated on posts, suggesting it is designed for durability and ease of cleaning. The overall design is functional, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on practicality for housing cattle.
description: A rectangular cowshed with a red gable roof, tan wooden siding, large barn doors, and a raised entry with stairs. The structure is practical and elevated on posts, with windows and minimal decorative features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long facade with multiple large barn doors and a partial overhang on the left.
description_view_1: End view featuring a raised entry door accessed by a wooden staircase.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a continuous gable roof and no visible doors or windows, but a small overhang on the right.
description_view_3: End view with two rectangular windows and the building elevated on posts.
attribute_door_style: single wooden plank door with external staircase, multiple large sliding barn doors
attribute_window_presence: two rectangular windows on one end
